goal_spec_json.goal_statement: Sleep a minimum of 6 hours consistently every night

morning_briefing.morning_message:
Morning Ameena.

You're feeling fantastic today — that 100 score and high energy mode is real. Let's start with last night: you slept **6.93 hours** with 93% efficiency and very little waking. That's your longest, most efficient sleep recently, and your body's recovery signals are showing a dramatic rebound — your HRV hit **120**, which is exceptionally strong. That's a great foundation.

But there's a striking tension here. Yesterday, despite your body recovering well, your subjective energy was critically low at **30**, focus at **39**, and stress was rising. This is the 4th day of this mismatch: your physiological readiness is climbing while your internal experience feels depleted. Your activity also dipped yesterday — MVPA was just 16 minutes, and you were more sedentary. The data suggests your body might be using this lower activity to recover, but it's leaving you feeling drained.

Today's theme is about bridging that gap. Your actions are designed to protect that strong recovery while gently addressing the internal strain — using tiny breaks to manage stress and planning your nutrition to support your energy.

You've built a solid recovery base; today's about making sure your inner experience matches it.

assumptions: User can adjust evening schedule for earlier eating
User has phone/alarm device for external cues
User has access to protein-rich foods
User can take brief work breaks
Journal missingness may hide contextual stressors
soft_constraints: caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits
consider gentle activity to counter sedentary trend
maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min) to support sleep
leverage high subjective energy for sleep routine implementation

generated_actions.title: When you feel tension rising this afternoon, pause for 2 minutes of box breathing
generated_actions.rationale: Your subjective wellbeing shows severe strain: stress score was elevated yesterday (41) and rising (+0.57 slope), while coping capacity is critically low (39) and sharply declining (-9.87 slope). With hard constraints 'tight schedule' and 'high workload', this micro-intervention uses implementation intention ('when you feel tension') to disrupt stress accumulation and support the 'high mood, low function' paradox noted in the holistic report.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you use the box breathing technique when you felt tension today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports using the breathing technique at least once when stress arose

assumptions: You can take brief pauses during work
generated_actions.description: If you notice stress or tension building during your high-workload afternoon, immediately stop and do 4 rounds of box breathing: inhale 4 seconds, hold 4, exhale 4, hold 4. Focus on your breath.

generated_actions.title: After your morning coffee, add a protein source to your lunch plan
generated_actions.rationale: Nutrition status is below_avg with a protein deficit (58g yesterday, -1.07 fast z-score) and declining intake (-7.79 slope). Meal composition included processed foods (croissant, chips, cookie). Habit-stacking lunch planning to your morning coffee routine directly addresses the 'nutrition_gap' risk flag and the soft constraint 'caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits', aiming to stabilize energy and support recovery.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did planning your protein-rich lunch after coffee help you eat better today?
evaluation.success_definition: Daily protein intake reaches at least 80g (addressing the 58g deficit) AND user reports the planning was helpful

assumptions: You have access to protein-rich foods
You can plan your lunch
generated_actions.description: Right after finishing your morning coffee, decide on a lunch that includes a substantial protein source (like grilled chicken, eggs, or lentils) to be eaten before 1:00 PM. Yesterday's lunch was a stuffed focaccia sandwich – aim for more protein today.

generated_actions.title: Set a 7:30 PM 'eating cutoff' alarm to finish dinner by 7:00 PM
generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday's last meal was unusually early (690 minutes/~11:30 AM), well below baselines, and eating window was short (205 minutes). This aligns with excellent sleep efficiency (93.27%) and low wake ratio (6.73%) per the 'improved_nutrition_timing_with_sleep_quality' cross-domain signal. Reinforcing this pattern with an external cue directly supports your sleep goal domain, the 'sleep_consistency_recovery' priority, and the soft constraint 'maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min) to support sleep'.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did your 7:30 PM alarm help you stop eating and start winding down earlier?
evaluation.success_definition: Last meal occurs before 7:00 PM (time_last_meal_minutes < 1020) AND user reports the alarm was helpful

assumptions: You have a phone or alarm device
You can adjust your evening schedule
generated_actions.description: Set an alarm on your phone for 7:30 PM tonight. When it rings, that's your signal to stop any eating and begin winding down. Aim to actually finish dinner by 7:00 PM local time.

must_avoid: continuing screen use after the alarm

generated_actions.title: After 60 minutes of focused work, take a 5-minute walk or stretch break
generated_actions.rationale: Activity profile shows concerning decline: MVPA was critically low yesterday (16 minutes) and sharply declining (-13.88 slope), sedentary time is rising (+14.67 slope), and focus is critically low (39) and declining (-8.88 slope). This tiny break directly addresses the 'activity_decline_amid_recovery_rebound' cross-domain signal and the soft constraint 'consider gentle activity to counter sedentary trend' within your 'tight schedule' and 'high workload' hard constraints, while leveraging your high subjective energy mode.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you take at least one 5-minute movement break after prolonged focused work today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports taking at least one movement break (walk or stretch) after 60+ minutes of continuous work

assumptions: You can take short breaks during work
You have space to walk or stretch
generated_actions.description: When you complete 60 minutes of continuous focused work, immediately stand up and either walk around for 5 minutes or do a full-body stretch sequence. Look away from your screen and move your body.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: After your morning coffee, take 5 minutes of gentle stretching or mobility work
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Physiological recovery signals are critically depressed: HRV was zero yesterday (-92.39 fast z-score) and physio proxy score is 2 (-47.37 fast z-score), sharply declining. Despite your high self-reported energy (100), this indicates severe internal strain. Gentle movement after a routine activity (morning coffee) promotes parasympathetic activation without taxing your system, directly addressing the 'low_recovery' risk flag and 'high_activity_with_critical_recovery_mismatch' cross-domain signal.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you complete 5 minutes of gentle stretching after your morning coffee?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing at least 5 minutes of light stretching/mobility after coffee

assumptions: You have space to move
You can spare 5 minutes after coffee
previous_day_generated_actions.description: Right after you finish your morning coffee, spend 5 minutes doing gentle stretches or mobility exercises like leg swings, shoulder rolls, and torso twists. Keep it light, no heavy exertion.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: Set a 9:00 PM 'last snack' alarm and finish eating by 7:30 PM
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday's last meal was at 22:39 (1359 minutes) and eating window was 859 minutes (~14.3 hours), trending later (+11.69 slope). This aligns with elevated sleep wake ratio (11.26%) and reduced efficiency (88.74%). The 'late_nutrition_with_sleep_fragmentation' cross-domain signal suggests timing mismatch may contribute to sleep fragmentation. This directly addresses the 'nutrition_gap' and 'sleep_debt' risk flags and your sleep goal domain.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did your 9:00 PM alarm help you stop eating and start winding down earlier?
evaluation.success_definition: Last meal occurs before 7:30 PM (time_last_meal_minutes < 1050) AND user reports the alarm was helpful

assumptions: You have a phone or alarm device
You can adjust your evening schedule
previous_day_generated_actions.description: Set an alarm for 9:00 PM tonight. When it rings, that's your cue to stop any eating and begin winding down. Aim to actually finish dinner by 7:30 PM local time.

must_avoid: continuing screen use after the alarm

previous_day_generated_actions.title: When you feel tension building in the afternoon, pause for 3 rounds of 4-7-8 breathing
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Stress score was elevated yesterday (35) and rising (+1.31 slope), while workload perception is high (71) and also rising (+5.12 slope). With tight schedule and high workload hard constraints, this micro-intervention uses implementation intention ('when you feel tension') to disrupt stress accumulation, supporting the 'stress_coping' domain status and leveraging your high focus (91) for intentional regulation.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you use the 4-7-8 breathing technique when you felt tension today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports using the breathing technique at least once when stress arose

assumptions: You can take brief pauses during work
previous_day_generated_actions.description: If you notice stress or tension rising during your high-workload afternoon, immediately pause and do 3 rounds of 4-7-8 breathing: inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 7, exhale for 8.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: After brushing your teeth tonight, dim lights and avoid screens for 15 minutes
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Sleep efficiency was below baseline yesterday (88.74, -3.17 fast z-score) and declining (-1.06 slope), with elevated wake ratio (11.26%, +1.06 slope). Habit-stacking to your existing bedtime routine (brushing teeth) creates a consistent wind-down to improve sleep continuity, directly addressing the 'sleep_debt' risk flag and supporting your sleep goal domain.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you spend 15 minutes screen-free with dim lights after brushing teeth?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing at least 15 minutes of screen-free wind-down with dim lighting

assumptions: You have access to dim lighting
You can avoid screens for 15 minutes
previous_day_generated_actions.description: Immediately after brushing your teeth before bed, dim the lights in your bedroom and avoid phones/computers for 15 minutes. You can read a book, listen to calm music, or do light tidying.

previous_day_briefing.morning_message:
Morning Ameena.

You said you feel like a perfect 100 this morning — that's wonderful to hear. Let's look at what your body is telling us. Last night you got 6.6 hours of sleep, which is right around your usual, but your sleep efficiency was down at 88.7% with 11.3% of the night spent awake — that's more fragmented than normal. Yesterday's sleep quality rating was a 71, reflecting how you felt about the night before.

Here's the deeper story: yesterday you were incredibly active — 16k steps and 92 minutes of MVPA — but your physiological recovery signals collapsed. Your HRV hit zero, and your internal recovery score dropped to a 2/100. That's a massive mismatch: your body is screaming for rest while your mind and behavior are pushing hard. Your subjective energy was critically low at 37, even though your focus remained sky-high at 91. The late eating (last meal at 10:39 PM) likely didn't help that sleep fragmentation either.

Today isn't about adding more output; it's about honoring that recovery gap. Your body needs gentle signals to restore itself, not more strain.

Your actions today are all about giving your system the repair space it's asking for, without adding pressure.

Let today be the day you listen to what your physiology has been shouting.

evidence_quotes: I spent most of the day out today, which was super fun, but i also didn't get anything productive done

meal_descriptions.description: Butter croissant and a small iced latte
Stuffed foccacia sandwich and potato chips on the side
1 small beef taco and a spoon of truffle and chicken risotto and a fried potato.
